heroHeadline: Myanmar Junta Launches 'Civilian' Rebrand as Airstrikes Kill Hundreds and Resistance Holds Ground
heroSubtitle: Five years after the February 2021 coup, the SAC junta convened a junta-packed parliament on March 16 as part of a staged 'civilian' rebrand, condemned as illegitimate by the NUG, ASEAN, and international experts. Russian-supplied drones and 'meat assault' tactics from Ukraine now shape SAC operations. ACLED recorded 5,912 airstrikes since 2021, with air-attack deaths up 52% in 2025. Over 3.6 million remain displaced; 22,800+ political prisoners are still held despite a March 2 mass amnesty of 10,000+.
footerNote: Data sourced from AAPP, ACLED, UN OCHA, Irrawaddy, RFA, Myanmar Now, DVB, Al Jazeera, Human Rights Watch, Crisis Group, Myanmar Peace Monitor, and official NUG, KNU, and AA statements.
